goal: Sub-agent-per-task: server.py /ask loops through plan tasks, spawning a fresh claude --print subprocess per task; ask.md updated for single-task execution; TOKEN_USE.md marked done

tasks.name: Update ask.md for single-task mode
tasks.action: Clarify that each invocation executes exactly one task. Add: when plan_current returns no task, output exactly `DONE` and stop. Remove any implication of looping.
tasks.verify: ask.md contains DONE marker instruction and single-task framing
tasks.done: ask.md instructs agent to output DONE when no task found, and does not imply it loops

tasks.name: Add task-loop to server.py /ask
tasks.action: Replace the single subprocess.run in /ask with a loop (max 50 iterations). Each iteration spawns a fresh claude --print with the same system prompt and original query. After each response, check if response contains the DONE marker; if so, break. Collect all per-task responses. Return combined response. Keep /plan endpoint unchanged.
tasks.verify: server.py /ask endpoint has a loop that spawns a fresh subprocess per iteration and stops on DONE
tasks.done: server.py /ask loops, spawning one subprocess per task, terminating on DONE or error

tasks.name: Update TOKEN_USE.md
tasks.action: In Implementation Tasks table, mark task #10 (Add sub-agent-per-task mode) as Done. In section 3.2 Prompt Changes, mark the sub-agent row as implemented. Update the Prioritized Action Items table in section 2 (P1 sub-agent item) status from Open to Done.
tasks.verify: TOKEN_USE.md shows task #10 as Done and P1 sub-agent item as Done
tasks.done: TOKEN_USE.md accurately reflects that sub-agent-per-task is implemented
